441	class ForwardFlags:
442	    """Per-forward runtime flags with one API and two backings.
443	
444	    Flags read only from eager Python are backed by context variables, so
445	    nested scopes and threads stay isolated (a new thread sees the defaults).
446	    Flags that are read or written *inside torch.compile-traced model code*
447	    (``_GRAPH_VISIBLE``) are backed by plain dict slots instead: dynamo
448	    cannot trace ``ContextVar.get``/``set``, while plain reads it guards on
449	    — the storage form these flags had before joining the tier. Their
450	    writers and readers are single-threaded per process (TBO interleaves
451	    ubatches on one thread; attention-TP input scattering excludes TBO), so
452	    context isolation is not needed for correctness.
453	
454	    ``scoped(**kw)`` — the one regular write path — restores on exit for
455	    both backings. ``set()`` exists for the legacy unscoped setters' shims.
456	    """
457	
458	    _DEFAULTS = {
459	        "multi_stream": False,
460	        "moe_output_buffer": None,
461	        # Attention-TP input-scattering (set per forward by
462	        # AttnTpContext.maybe_input_scattered / set_attn_inputs).
463	        "attn_input_scattered": False,
464	        "attn_inputs": None,
465	        # Sticky across forwards: every ForwardBatch construction writes it;
466	        # graph runners force False around capture.
467	        "is_extend_in_batch": False,
468	        # Per-layer MLP collective control (set by decoder via scoped()
469	        # around the MLP / MoE / hybrid mixer call).
470	        # fuse_mlp_allreduce: next residual+LN absorbs the post-MLP all-reduce.
471	        # mlp_reduce_scatter: postprocess will reduce-scatter (skip MLP AR).
472	        # flashinfer_trtllm_bypass: deepseek dual-stream graph topk bypass.
473	        "fuse_mlp_allreduce": False,
474	        "mlp_reduce_scatter": False,
475	        "flashinfer_trtllm_bypass": False,
476	    }
477	
478	    # Read/written inside compiled graphs (vocab embedding, communicator,
479	    # EP dispatch, DP gather/scatter, MLP/MoE skip-AR): plain-slot backed.
480	    # Before moving a flag out of this set, prove no read/write site sits
481	    # under torch.compile.
482	    _GRAPH_VISIBLE = frozenset(
483	        {
484	            "attn_input_scattered",
485	            "attn_inputs",
486	            "is_extend_in_batch",
487	            "fuse_mlp_allreduce",
488	            "mlp_reduce_scatter",
489	            "flashinfer_trtllm_bypass",
490	        }
491	    )
492	
493	    __slots__ = ("_vars", "_plain")
494	
495	    def __init__(self):
496	        import contextvars
497	
498	        object.__setattr__(
499	            self,
500	            "_plain",
501	            {
502	                name: default
503	                for name, default in self._DEFAULTS.items()
504	                if name in self._GRAPH_VISIBLE
505	            },
506	        )
507	        object.__setattr__(
508	            self,
509	            "_vars",
510	            {
511	                name: contextvars.ContextVar(f"forward.{name}", default=default)
512	                for name, default in self._DEFAULTS.items()
513	                if name not in self._GRAPH_VISIBLE
514	            },
515	        )
516	
517	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
518	        plain = self._plain
519	        if name in plain:
520	            return plain[name]
521	        try:
522	            return self._vars[name].get()
523	        except KeyError:
524	            raise AttributeError(
525	                f"ForwardFlags has no flag '{name}' (flags are declared in "
526	                "ForwardFlags._DEFAULTS; check for typos)"
527	            ) from None
528	
529	    def __setattr__(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:
530	        raise AttributeError(
531	            "ForwardFlags is written through scoped(**kw) (or the legacy "
532	            "set() shim), never by attribute assignment"
533	        )
534	
535	    def set(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:
536	        """Unscoped write for legacy setter shims; persists until the next
537	        write (current context only, for contextvar-backed flags)."""
538	        if name in self._plain:
539	            self._plain[name] = value
540	        else:
541	            self._vars[name].set(value)
542	
543	    @contextmanager
544	    def scoped(self, **kwargs):
545	        """Set flags for the current scope, restoring on exit. Transactional
546	        (keys validated before any write) and exception-safe."""
547	        unknown = set(kwargs) - set(self._DEFAULTS)
548	        if unknown:
549	            raise ValueError(f"unknown forward flag(s): {sorted(unknown)}")
550	        plain_saved = [
551	            (name, self._plain[name]) for name in kwargs if name in self._plain
552	        ]
553	        tokens = []
554	        for name, value in kwargs.items():
555	            if name in self._plain:
556	                self._plain[name] = value
557	            else:
558	                tokens.append((self._vars[name], self._vars[name].set(value)))
559	        try:
560	            yield self
561	        finally:
562	            for var, token in reversed(tokens):
563	                var.reset(token)
564	            for name, value in reversed(plain_saved):
565	                self._plain[name] = value
566	
567	
568	class _ConfigBag:
569	    """A resolved-config namespace bag.
570	
571	    Values are snapshotted from ``server_args`` at ``publish`` and this bag is
572	    the **single source of truth** for its fields thereafter. Read is plain
573	    attribute access; the bag is read-only by bare assignment. The sanctioned
574	    writers are ``get_context().override(source, ...)`` (permanent) and
575	    the scoped ``.override(**kw)`` context manager (tests). Sub-namespaces
576	    (e.g. ``exec.moe``) are nested ``_ConfigBag`` instances reached by attribute.
577	    """
578	
579	    __slots__ = ("_path", "_fields", "_subs")
580	
581	    def __init__(self, path: str):
582	        object.__setattr__(self, "_path", path)
583	        object.__setattr__(self, "_fields", {})  # {leaf: value}
584	        object.__setattr__(self, "_subs", {})  # {subname: _ConfigBag}
585	
586	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
587	        # Reached only when ``name`` is not a real attribute (slot).
588	        fields = object.__getattribute__(self, "_fields")
589	        if name in fields:
590	            return fields[name]
591	        subs = object.__getattribute__(self, "_subs")
592	        if name in subs:
593	            return subs[name]
594	        path = object.__getattribute__(self, "_path")
595	        raise AttributeError(f"config namespace {path!r} has no leaf/subgroup {name!r}")
596	
597	    def __setattr__(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:
598	        raise AttributeError(
599	            f"config namespace {self._path!r} is read-only; write via "
600	            "get_context().override(source, ...) or the scoped .override(**kw)"
601	        )
602	
603	    def _set(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:
604	        """Internal write (publish + override) that bypasses the read-only guard."""
605	        object.__getattribute__(self, "_fields")[name] = value
606	
607	    def __contains__(self, name: str) -> bool:
608	        return name in object.__getattribute__(self, "_fields")
609	
610	    @contextmanager
611	    def override(self, **kwargs):
612	        """Scoped, transactional test-only override of this bag's own leaves
613	        (keys validated before any write; restored on exit)."""
614	        fields = object.__getattribute__(self, "_fields")
615	        unknown = set(kwargs) - set(fields)
616	        if unknown:
617	            path = object.__getattribute__(self, "_path")
618	            raise ValueError(f"unknown config leaf for {path!r}: {sorted(unknown)}")
619	        saved = {name: fields[name] for name in kwargs}
620	        fields.update(kwargs)
621	        try:
622	            yield self
623	        finally:
624	            fields.update(saved)
625	
626	
627	def _build_config_bags(server_args: Any) -> dict:
628	    """Snapshot resolved ``server_args`` into the namespace bag tree, driven by
629	    the ``NS(...)`` metadata on the dataclass fields. Returns
630	    ``{top_level_name: _ConfigBag}``, arbitrarily nested (``exec.moe.eplb.…``).
631	    Only dataclass fields carry ``NS`` markers, so derived properties/methods are
632	    naturally excluded (they stay on the bag). A name used as both a leaf and a
633	    subgroup at the same level is a hard error — no silent shadowing."""
634	    from sglang.srt.arg_groups.arg_utils import namespace_of
635	
636	    _MISSING = object()
637	    tops: dict = {}
638	    for field, path in namespace_of(type(server_args)).items():
639	        value = getattr(server_args, field, _MISSING)
640	        if value is _MISSING:
641	            # Every NS-declared field is a dataclass field, so a resolved config
642	            # always carries it; a miss means a malformed/partial config object
643	            # was published. Fail loud here rather than silently omitting the
644	            # leaf (which surfaces later as a confusing "not a published leaf").
645	            raise AttributeError(
646	                f"config field {field!r} is declared NS({path!r}) but absent from "
647	                f"the published {type(server_args).__name__}; cannot project its bag leaf"
648	            )
649	        parts = path.split(".")
650	        bag = tops.get(parts[0])
651	        if bag is None:
652	            bag = tops[parts[0]] = _ConfigBag(parts[0])
653	        for depth in range(1, len(parts)):
654	            name = parts[depth]
655	            if name in object.__getattribute__(bag, "_fields"):
656	                raise ValueError(
657	                    f"config namespace collision: {'.'.join(parts[: depth + 1])!r} "
658	                    "is declared as both a leaf and a subgroup"
659	                )
660	            subs = object.__getattribute__(bag, "_subs")
661	            child = subs.get(name)
662	            if child is None:
663	                child = subs[name] = _ConfigBag(".".join(parts[: depth + 1]))
664	            bag = child
665	        if field in object.__getattribute__(bag, "_subs"):
666	            raise ValueError(
667	                f"config namespace collision: leaf {field!r} under {path!r} "
668	                "clashes with a subgroup of the same name"
669	            )
670	        bag._set(field, value)
671	    return tops
672	
673	
674	class RuntimeContext:
675	    """Container for the structured runtime accessors; exposes ``parallel``,
676	    ``server_args``, the resolved config namespace bags, ``flags``,
677	    ``resources``, and ``forward``."""
678	
679	    __slots__ = (
680	        "parallel",
681	        "_server_args",
682	        "_config_bags",
683	        "_overrides_log",
684	        "flags",
685	        "resources",
686	        "forward",
687	    )
688	
689	    def __init__(self, parallel: ParallelContext):
690	        self.parallel = parallel
691	        self._server_args: ServerArgs | None = None
692	        self._config_bags: dict | None = None
693	        self._overrides_log: list = []
694	        self.flags = Flags()
695	        self.resources = Resources()
696	        self.forward = ForwardFlags()
697	
698	    def get_stream(self, name: str) -> Any:
699	        """Named process-level CUDA side stream: get-or-create, shared by
700	        name (the keyed-lazy pattern of the persistent buffers). Creation is
701	        a driver call that must stay outside cuda-graph capture — call sites
702	        lease their stream at init/warmup time."""
703	        stream = self.resources.streams.get(name)
704	        if stream is None:
705	            import torch
706	
707	            stream = torch.cuda.Stream()
708	            self.resources.streams[name] = stream
709	        return stream
710	
711	    def set_stream(self, name: str, stream: Any) -> Any:
712	        """Install (or replace) the named stream — explicit injection for
713	        tests and backends that bring their own stream."""
714	        self.resources.streams[name] = stream
715	        return stream
716	
717	    def get_buffer(self, name: str, factory: Any) -> Any:
718	        """Named process-level persistent buffer: get-or-create via
719	        ``factory()``, shared by name (the keyed-lazy pattern of the
720	        persistent buffers / named streams)."""
721	        buf = self.resources.buffers.get(name)
722	        if buf is None:
723	            buf = factory()
724	            self.resources.buffers[name] = buf
725	        return buf
726	
727	    @property
728	    def server_args(self) -> ServerArgs:
729	        """The process-wide ``ServerArgs`` (context-owned slot)."""
730	        server_args = self._server_args
731	        if server_args is None:
732	            # Verbatim legacy message: tests and user scripts may match on it.
733	            raise ValueError("Global server args is not set yet!")
734	        return server_args
735	
736	    def set_server_args(self, server_args: ServerArgs) -> None:
737	        """Publish the process-wide ``ServerArgs`` into the context-owned slot.
738	
739	        Overwrite-allowed: a re-publish replaces the slot (test kits re-publish
740	        per test; production ordering discipline lives at the call-sites, e.g.
741	        the draft-worker guard in ``ModelRunner.__init__``). The published
742	        object already carries the resolved configuration (declarations
743	        materialize at the end of ``__post_init__``).
744	        """
745	        # Seed the capture tier for the new lifecycle (defaults for sentinel
746	        # and mock publishes, which carry no config).
747	        self.flags.capture.enable_torch_compile = getattr(
748	            server_args, "enable_torch_compile", False
749	        )
750	        self._server_args = server_args
751	        # Snapshot resolved config into the namespace bags (the single source of
752	        # truth for config reads). Driven by NS(...) metadata; a mock/partial
753	        # config with no NS markers yields an empty tree (no bags projected).
754	        self._config_bags = _build_config_bags(server_args)
755	        # Wire the parallel config leaves onto the live wrapper (config-only
756	        # leaves like pp_max_micro_batch_size are served via ParallelContext
757	        # __getattr__; live topology properties still win by name).
758	        self.parallel._config = self._config_bags.get("parallel")
759	        # Fresh config lifecycle: prior override provenance no longer applies.
760	        self._overrides_log = []
761	
762	    def config_bag(self, name: str) -> _ConfigBag:
763	        """Return the top-level config namespace bag (``device`` / ``model`` /
764	        ``exec`` / ``schedule`` / ``memory`` / ``spec`` / ``lora`` / ``mm`` /
765	        ``disagg`` / ``serving`` / ``observability``). Fails closed until
766	        ``publish`` / ``set_server_args`` has projected it."""
767	        bags = self._config_bags
768	        if not bags or name not in bags:
769	            raise ValueError(f"config namespace {name!r} not published")
770	        return bags[name]
771	
772	    def override(self, source: str, **fields) -> None:
773	        """The business mutation entry: write resolved config
774	        leaves onto the namespace bags — the single source of truth. It does
775	        **not** touch ``server_args`` (the pristine startup record) and there is
776	        no write-through, so the old "wrote one store, read another" desync class
777	        cannot occur.
778	
779	        Each flat field name is routed to its bag by the ``NS`` metadata (flat
780	        names are unique across namespaces). Validation is all-or-nothing: an
781	        unknown / unprojected field aborts before any write. ``source`` is
782	        recorded for provenance / reproduction.
783	        """
784	        if not fields:
785	            return
786	        bags = self._config_bags
787	        if bags is None:
788	            raise ValueError("config not published; cannot override")
789	        from sglang.srt.arg_groups.arg_utils import namespace_of
790	
791	        nsmap = namespace_of(type(self._server_args))
792	        targets = []  # (bag, leaf, value) — resolved before any write
793	        for name, value in fields.items():
794	            path = nsmap.get(name)
795	            if path is None:
796	                raise ValueError(
797	                    f"override: unknown config field {name!r} (no NS namespace) — "
798	                    "not a resolved config leaf"
799	                )
800	            parts = path.split(".")
801	            bag = bags.get(parts[0])
802	            if bag is None:
803	                raise ValueError(f"override: namespace {parts[0]!r} not published")
804	            for seg in parts[1:]:
805	                bag = object.__getattribute__(bag, "_subs").get(seg)
806	                if bag is None:
807	                    raise ValueError(
808	                        f"override: subgroup {seg!r} missing under {path!r}"
809	                    )
810	            if name not in bag:
811	                raise ValueError(f"override: field {name!r} not projected on {path!r}")
812	            targets.append((bag, name, value))
813	        for bag, name, value in targets:
814	            bag._set(name, value)
815	        self._overrides_log.append((source, dict(fields)))
816	
817	    def overrides_log(self) -> list:
818	        """Provenance of post-publish ``override`` calls: ``[(source, {field: value})]``."""
819	        return list(self._overrides_log)
820	
821	    def resolved_server_args_dict(self, base: dict | None = None) -> dict:
822	        """Serialize the *resolved* config: the pristine ``server_args`` fields
823	        with every post-publish ``override`` overlaid.
824	
825	        Reporting endpoints (``/server_info``, ``get_internal_state``) surface
826	        the config the process is *currently* running, not the startup record,
827	        so they read this rather than serializing ``server_args`` directly —
828	        otherwise runtime updates (weight version, model path, tunables set via
829	        ``/set_internal_state``) never show up in the readback.
830	
831	        ``base`` defaults to ``dict(vars(server_args))`` (matching the legacy
832	        ``vars`` dump); pass ``dataclasses.asdict(server_args)`` when nested
833	        dataclass fields must be expanded first (``/server_info``). Override
834	        leaves are flat ``ServerArgs`` field names, so overlaying them onto the
835	        top level of either base is exact.
836	        """
837	        d = dict(vars(self.server_args)) if base is None else dict(base)
838	        for _source, fields in self._overrides_log:
839	            d.update(fields)
840	        return d
841	
842	    def override_server_args(self, **fields) -> _ServerArgsOverride:
843	        """Test-only scoped override for the config tier — the sibling of
844	        ``get_parallel().override()`` and the flag groups' ``override()``:
845	        tests force execution paths by overriding the context instead of
846	        hand-building config objects.
847	
848	        ``install()`` (or entering it as a context manager) publishes a fresh
849	        dummy-boundary ``ServerArgs`` carrying ``fields`` and returns it;
850	        ``restore()`` (or exiting) reinstates whatever the slot held before.
851	
852	        Transitional — to be deprecated: it exists because production code
853	        still branches on raw ``server_args`` fields at runtime, so forcing a
854	        path needs a full config in the slot. As those readers migrate onto
855	        the named runtime tiers (flags / resources / forward), prefer the
856	        finer-grained overrides; once they cover the branching surface this
857	        override loses its clients and goes away.
858	        """
859	        return _ServerArgsOverride(self, fields)
860	
861	
862	class _ServerArgsOverride:
863	    """Scoped config override (see ``RuntimeContext.override_server_args``).
864	
865	    Deliberately a plain class rather than a generator context manager:
866	    fixtures that live for a whole test case install the override without a
867	    ``with`` block, and a suspended generator would run its restore whenever
868	    the garbage collector closes it — un-publishing the active config at a
869	    nondeterministic point.
870	    """
871	
872	    __slots__ = ("_context", "_fields", "_previous", "_previous_capture", "_installed")
873	
874	    def __init__(self, context: RuntimeContext, fields: dict):
875	        self._context = context
876	        self._fields = fields
877	        self._previous: ServerArgs | None = None
878	        self._previous_capture = False
879	        self._installed = False
880	
881	    def install(self) -> ServerArgs:
882	        """Publish a fresh dummy-boundary ``ServerArgs`` carrying the
883	        overrides (written through ``ServerArgs.override`` for provenance);
884	        returns the published instance."""
885	        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
886	
887	        assert not self._installed, "override_server_args already installed"
888	        self._previous = self._context._server_args
889	        self._previous_capture = self._context.flags.capture.enable_torch_compile
890	        server_args = ServerArgs(model_path="dummy")
891	        if self._fields:
892	            server_args.override(source="test-override", **self._fields)
893	        # The dummy boundary skips materialization, which would leave the
894	        # strict mutation guard unarmed on the published object — mark it
895	        # materialized so bare post-publish writes raise like they do on a
896	        # fully resolved config.
897	        object.__setattr__(server_args, "_declarations_materialized", True)
898	        self._context.set_server_args(server_args)
899	        self._installed = True
900	        return server_args
901	
902	    def restore(self) -> None:
903	        """Reinstate the previously published config (or the empty slot)."""
904	        if not self._installed:
905	            return
906	        self._installed = False
907	        previous, self._previous = self._previous, None
908	        if previous is None:
909	            self._context._server_args = None
910	        else:
911	            self._context.set_server_args(previous)
912	        # set_server_args reseeds the capture tier from the published object
913	        # (and the empty-slot path does not touch it at all); the snapshot
914	        # puts back the exact pre-install runtime state either way.
915	        self._context.flags.capture.enable_torch_compile = self._previous_capture
916	
917	    def __enter__(self) -> ServerArgs:
918	        return self.install()
919	
920	    def __exit__(self, *exc) -> None:
921	        self.restore()
922	
923	
924	_PARALLEL = ParallelContext()
925	_CONTEXT = RuntimeContext(parallel=_PARALLEL)
926	
927	
928	def get_context() -> RuntimeContext:
929	    return _CONTEXT
930	
931	
932	def get_parallel() -> ParallelContext:
933	    return _PARALLEL
934	
935	
936	def get_server_args() -> ServerArgs:
937	    return _CONTEXT.server_args
938	
939	
940	def get_flags() -> Flags:
941	    return _CONTEXT.flags
942	
943	
944	def get_resources() -> Resources:
945	    return _CONTEXT.resources
946	
947	
948	def get_forward() -> ForwardFlags:
949	    return _CONTEXT.forward
950	
951	
952	# --- Resolved config namespaces -------------------------
953	# Each returns the top-level snapshot bag; reads are `get_exec().moe.field` etc.
954	# All fail with ValueError("... not published") until publish has projected them.
955	# ``parallel`` config leaves are served by ``get_parallel()`` (live wrapper);
956	# their config-bag wiring is a scoped follow-up.
957	def get_device() -> _ConfigBag:
958	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("device")
959	
960	
961	def get_model() -> _ConfigBag:
962	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("model")
963	
964	
965	def get_exec() -> _ConfigBag:
966	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("exec")
967	
968	
969	def get_schedule() -> _ConfigBag:
970	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("schedule")
971	
972	
973	def get_memory() -> _ConfigBag:
974	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("memory")
975	
976	
977	def get_spec() -> _ConfigBag:
978	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("spec")
979	
980	
981	def get_lora() -> _ConfigBag:
982	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("lora")
983	
984	
985	def get_mm() -> _ConfigBag:
986	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("mm")
987	
988	
989	def get_disagg() -> _ConfigBag:
990	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("disagg")
991	
992	
993	def get_serving() -> _ConfigBag:
994	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("serving")
995	
996	
997	def get_observability() -> _ConfigBag:
998	    return _CONTEXT.config_bag("observability")
999	
1000	
1001	def get_stream(name: str) -> Any:
1002	    return _CONTEXT.get_stream(name)
1003	
1004	
1005	def set_stream(name: str, stream: Any) -> Any:
1006	    return _CONTEXT.set_stream(name, stream)
1007	
1008	
1009	def get_buffer(name: str, factory: Any) -> Any:
1010	    return _CONTEXT.get_buffer(name, factory)
1011	
1012	
1013	_GLOBAL_DWDP_MANAGER: Any = None
1014	
1015	
1016	def get_global_dwdp_manager() -> Any:
1017	    return _GLOBAL_DWDP_MANAGER
1018	
1019	
1020	def set_global_dwdp_manager(manager: Any) -> None:
1021	    global _GLOBAL_DWDP_MANAGER
1022	    _GLOBAL_DWDP_MANAGER = manager
1023	
1024	
1025	def reset_context() -> None:
1026	    """Clear the context-owned store (unit-test teardown): drop the published
1027	    ``server_args`` and install fresh ``Flags`` and ``Resources``.
1028	
1029	    Wrapper subsystems (``parallel``) hold no state and are unaffected.
1030	    """
1031	    _CONTEXT._server_args = None
1032	    _CONTEXT._config_bags = None
1033	    _CONTEXT._overrides_log = []
1034	    _CONTEXT.parallel._config = None
1035	    _CONTEXT.flags = Flags()
1036	    _CONTEXT.resources = Resources()
1037	    _CONTEXT.forward = ForwardFlags()
1038	    set_global_dwdp_manager(None)

# Use `msgspec.Struct`, not `@dataclass`

Define data containers as `msgspec.Struct`. Do not add new
`dataclasses.dataclass` (or `attrs`) — they weaken strict type checking and
don't translate cleanly for multi-language support (e.g. the planned Rust migration).

```python
import msgspec

class LoadSnapshot(msgspec.Struct):   # prefer frozen= and omit_defaults=; kw_only= as needed
    dp_rank: int = 0
    tokens: list[int] = []            # mutable defaults are safe
```

- Methods / `@classmethod` constructors go on the `Struct`; see
  `python/sglang/srt/managers/load_snapshot.py`.
- New code only. Existing `@dataclass` is grandfathered — migrate opportunistically
  while editing the file, not in drive-by sweeps.
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "**/*.py"
---

# Use `msgspec.Struct`, not `@dataclass`

Define data containers as `msgspec.Struct`. Do not add new
`dataclasses.dataclass` (or `attrs`) — they weaken strict type checking and
don't translate cleanly for multi-language support (e.g. the planned Rust migration).

```python
import msgspec

class LoadSnapshot(msgspec.Struct):   # prefer frozen= and omit_defaults=; kw_only= as needed
    dp_rank: int = 0
    tokens: list[int] = []            # mutable defaults are safe
```

- Methods / `@classmethod` constructors go on the `Struct`; see
  `python/sglang/srt/managers/load_snapshot.py`.
- New code only. Existing `@dataclass` is grandfathered — migrate opportunistically
  while editing the file, not in drive-by sweeps.

content.content:
# General Code Style

Default conventions for new and modified Python code. Prefer these unless there is a concrete reason not to; call out deviations in review.

- **Prefer stateless.** Favor pure functions over methods that mutate instance state; pass inputs in, return outputs out.
- **Prefer immutable.** Default to immutable data (frozen structs, tuples, read-only values); mutate only when there is a clear need.
- **Extract init-static values at construction.** When a derived value's inputs are frozen for the object's lifetime (typically configuration: constructor args, env vars, server args), compute it once in `__init__` and store it as a well-named attribute (`self.mtp_enabled`, `self.needs_cpu_seq_lens`); later code reads the attribute instead of re-deriving it. Input immutability is the hard precondition — if inputs can change, recompute in place or funnel mutation through a single override point (the frozen `ServerArgs.override()` pattern). If you can't give the value a meaningful name, the boundary is wrong — don't cache unnameable subexpressions.
- **Functions stay small.** Keep each function under ~100 LOC; split larger ones into named helpers.
- **Files stay small.** Keep each file under ~2k LOC; split larger modules along cohesive boundaries.
- **Core functions read like pseudocode.** The main / orchestration function of a unit should be short and read like algorithm pseudocode — push detail into well-named helpers so the top-level flow is obvious.
- **Avoid mixins.** Don't add behavior via mixin classes; prefer explicit composition (hold a collaborator and call it) or plain functions.
- **Prefer protected over public.** Default methods to protected (`_name`); expose only what callers actually use.
- **Prefer keyword arguments.** Call functions of 2+ args by keyword, and design APIs to be called that way.
- **Pass what you need, not the god object.** Give a callee the specific values it uses (by keyword), not a whole large object (`ModelRunner`, `Scheduler`); reserve passing the whole object for a leaf whose contract genuinely requires it. Even then, keep it read-only — read fields off it and return results for the caller to assign, rather than writing fields back through it.
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "**/*.py"
---

# General Code Style

Default conventions for new and modified Python code. Prefer these unless there is a concrete reason not to; call out deviations in review.

- **Prefer stateless.** Favor pure functions over methods that mutate instance state; pass inputs in, return outputs out.
- **Prefer immutable.** Default to immutable data (frozen structs, tuples, read-only values); mutate only when there is a clear need.
- **Extract init-static values at construction.** When a derived value's inputs are frozen for the object's lifetime (typically configuration: constructor args, env vars, server args), compute it once in `__init__` and store it as a well-named attribute (`self.mtp_enabled`, `self.needs_cpu_seq_lens`); later code reads the attribute instead of re-deriving it. Input immutability is the hard precondition — if inputs can change, recompute in place or funnel mutation through a single override point (the frozen `ServerArgs.override()` pattern). If you can't give the value a meaningful name, the boundary is wrong — don't cache unnameable subexpressions.
- **Functions stay small.** Keep each function under ~100 LOC; split larger ones into named helpers.
- **Files stay small.** Keep each file under ~2k LOC; split larger modules along cohesive boundaries.
- **Core functions read like pseudocode.** The main / orchestration function of a unit should be short and read like algorithm pseudocode — push detail into well-named helpers so the top-level flow is obvious.
- **Avoid mixins.** Don't add behavior via mixin classes; prefer explicit composition (hold a collaborator and call it) or plain functions.
- **Prefer protected over public.** Default methods to protected (`_name`); expose only what callers actually use.
- **Prefer keyword arguments.** Call functions of 2+ args by keyword, and design APIs to be called that way.
- **Pass what you need, not the god object.** Give a callee the specific values it uses (by keyword), not a whole large object (`ModelRunner`, `Scheduler`); reserve passing the whole object for a leaf whose contract genuinely requires it. Even then, keep it read-only — read fields off it and return results for the caller to assign, rather than writing fields back through it.

content.content:
# Mutate ScheduleBatch fields out of place

Never mutate a `ScheduleBatch` direct field in place — no `.extend()`/`.append()`,
`+=`/`-=`/`|=`, tensor `.add_()`/`.fill_()`, or slice/index assignment. Build the
new value and rebind the field:

```python
# Bad
self.reqs.extend(other.reqs)
self.seq_lens.add_(1)
self.extend_lens[i] -= encoder_len

# Good
self.reqs = self.reqs + other.reqs
self.seq_lens = self.seq_lens + 1
lens = self.extend_lens[:]; lens[i] -= encoder_len; self.extend_lens = lens  # loop a copy, rebind once
```

Why: `copy()` snapshots and the overlap scheduler's queued references rely on old
objects staying frozen (the prerequisite for the per-step immutable ScheduleBatch
refactor); derived values like `seq_lens_sum` only recompute safely when fields
are rebound.

Out of scope (mutation intended): penalizer buffers, CUDA graph static buffers,
`ForwardBatch` fields, attention-backend metadata.
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "python/sglang/srt/**/*.py"
---

# Mutate ScheduleBatch fields out of place

Never mutate a `ScheduleBatch` direct field in place — no `.extend()`/`.append()`,
`+=`/`-=`/`|=`, tensor `.add_()`/`.fill_()`, or slice/index assignment. Build the
new value and rebind the field:

```python
# Bad
self.reqs.extend(other.reqs)
self.seq_lens.add_(1)
self.extend_lens[i] -= encoder_len

# Good
self.reqs = self.reqs + other.reqs
self.seq_lens = self.seq_lens + 1
lens = self.extend_lens[:]; lens[i] -= encoder_len; self.extend_lens = lens  # loop a copy, rebind once
```

Why: `copy()` snapshots and the overlap scheduler's queued references rely on old
objects staying frozen (the prerequisite for the per-step immutable ScheduleBatch
refactor); derived values like `seq_lens_sum` only recompute safely when fields
are rebound.

Out of scope (mutation intended): penalizer buffers, CUDA graph static buffers,
`ForwardBatch` fields, attention-backend metadata.

content.content:
# Don't use `getattr` / `hasattr` for defensive access

Over-defensive `getattr(obj, "field", default)` / `hasattr(obj, "field")` hide
errors and defeat strict type checking. If a field is always present, accessing
it defensively is confusing and masks real bugs. Prefer:

1. **`isinstance` for type narrowing** — check the type, then access fields directly:

   ```python
   if (
       isinstance(obj, (TokenizedGenerateReqInput, TokenizedEmbeddingReqInput))
       and obj.mm_inputs
   ):
   ```
   (see `python/sglang/srt/managers/mm_utils.py`)

2. **Always set the field (to `None` if needed), then do a `None` check** — the
   field should always exist, so a `None` / non-`None` check is enough:

   ```python
   obj.field = None   # in __init__ / construction
   ...
   if obj.field is not None:
       ...
   ```

Bad — `server_args` always has `revision`, so `getattr` is misleading and swallows
a real `AttributeError` if the field is ever renamed:

```python
revision=getattr(server_args, "revision", None),   # BAD
revision=server_args.revision,                     # GOOD
```
(see `python/sglang/srt/managers/template_detection.py`)
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "**/*.py"
---

# Don't use `getattr` / `hasattr` for defensive access

Over-defensive `getattr(obj, "field", default)` / `hasattr(obj, "field")` hide
errors and defeat strict type checking. If a field is always present, accessing
it defensively is confusing and masks real bugs. Prefer:

1. **`isinstance` for type narrowing** — check the type, then access fields directly:

   ```python
   if (
       isinstance(obj, (TokenizedGenerateReqInput, TokenizedEmbeddingReqInput))
       and obj.mm_inputs
   ):
   ```
   (see `python/sglang/srt/managers/mm_utils.py`)

2. **Always set the field (to `None` if needed), then do a `None` check** — the
   field should always exist, so a `None` / non-`None` check is enough:

   ```python
   obj.field = None   # in __init__ / construction
   ...
   if obj.field is not None:
       ...
   ```

Bad — `server_args` always has `revision`, so `getattr` is misleading and swallows
a real `AttributeError` if the field is ever renamed:

```python
revision=getattr(server_args, "revision", None),   # BAD
revision=server_args.revision,                     # GOOD
```
(see `python/sglang/srt/managers/template_detection.py`)

content.content:
# `ForwardBatch.init_new` must not mutate the ScheduleBatch

`init_new` (and any ForwardBatch factory) treats the input `ScheduleBatch` as
read-only. Per-forward overrides go through the kw-only params of `init_new` /
`TpModelWorker.forward_batch_generation`, never a ScheduleBatch field. Batch-prep
writes (`out_cache_loc`, `seq_lens_*`, ...) before `init_new` are out of scope.

Tolerated exceptions (don't add new ones):

- `seq_lens_sum` backfill — the `seq_lens` family is slated for removal (→ kv-committed lengths).
- `sampling_info` sub-object writes (grammars, canary ids) — shared object, until the sampling forward-copy op.
- `_expand_mrope_from_input` memoizing `mrope_position_delta_repeated_cache` — pre-existing.

# `ForwardBatch.init_new` must not mutate the ScheduleBatch

`init_new` (and any ForwardBatch factory) treats the input `ScheduleBatch` as
read-only. Per-forward overrides go through the kw-only params of `init_new` /
`TpModelWorker.forward_batch_generation`, never a ScheduleBatch field. Batch-prep
writes (`out_cache_loc`, `seq_lens_*`, ...) before `init_new` are out of scope.

Tolerated exceptions (don't add new ones):

- `seq_lens_sum` backfill — the `seq_lens` family is slated for removal (→ kv-committed lengths).
- `sampling_info` sub-object writes (grammars, canary ids) — shared object, until the sampling forward-copy op.
- `_expand_mrope_from_input` memoizing `mrope_position_delta_repeated_cache` — pre-existing.

self.init_spec_aux_hidden_state()

        # Apply the rank zero filter to logger
        if server_args.show_time_cost:
            enable_show_time_cost()

        misc_utils.maybe_disable_chunked_prefix_cache(
            server_args=server_args,
            use_mla_backend=self.use_mla_backend,
            is_draft_worker=self.is_draft_worker,
        )

        # Set the global server_args in the scheduler process (target worker
        # only, so a draft init cannot clobber target-derived global state).
        if not self.is_draft_worker:
            set_global_server_args_for_scheduler(server_args)

        # Init OpenMP threads binding for CPU
        if self.device == "cpu":
            self.init_threads_binding()

        # Set float32 matmul precision
        if get_server_args().enable_tf32_matmul:
            torch.set_float32_matmul_precision("high")

file.content:
return fn


def _declaration_overlay(server_args: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Accumulated declared values: declarations never mutate
    ``server_args``, so mid-resolution readers overlay them from the
    declaration stash (last writer wins, like the gate)."""
    overlay: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    for _source, declared in getattr(server_args, "_resolved_overrides", None) or ():
        overlay.update(declared)
    return overlay


def run_post_process_pass(server_args: Any, fn: Callable[..., dict]) -> None:
    """Invoke one pass at its legacy handler slot.

    Evaluates the pass on the resolving state (a read-only view with the
    accumulated declarations overlaid from the stash) and appends its
    declaration to the stash. During ``__post_init__`` the fields stay
    untouched — ``materialize_declarations`` applies the whole stash once at
    the end of resolution; a pass invoked after materialization (a post-init
    slot) writes through immediately.
    """
    declared = fn(ResolvedView(server_args, overlay=_declaration_overlay(server_args)))
    if not isinstance(declared, dict):
        raise TypeError(
            f"post-process pass {fn.__qualname__} must return a dict, "
            f"got {type(declared).__name__}"
        )
    if declared:
        entry = (fn.__qualname__, dict(declared))
        stash = getattr(server_args, "_resolved_overrides", None)
        if stash is None:
            # Handlers hosting pass slots may be invoked directly on fixtures
            # that never ran the monolith dispatch (which owns the stash);
            # create it lazily. Real publishes always pass through the
            # dispatch first — the dispatch ASSIGNS the stash, so pass slots
            # must sit at or after it in __post_init__ order.
            stash = server_args._resolved_overrides = []
        stash.append(entry)
        validate_declarations(server_args, [entry])
        if getattr(server_args, "_declarations_materialized", False):
            _apply_fields(server_args, declared)


def _apply_fields(server_args: Any, fields: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Write fields on behalf of the pipeline (bypasses the strict bare-
    assignment guard that protects post-resolution mutation)."""
    object.__setattr__(server_args, "_in_override", True)
    try:
        for field, value in fields.items():
            setattr(server_args, field, value)
    finally:
        object.__setattr__(server_args, "_in_override", False)


def materialize_declarations(server_args: Any) -> None:
    """Apply the accumulated declarations onto ``server_args`` once, at the
    end of ``__post_init__`` (gate order: last writer wins). After this the
    fields carry the resolved configuration — every post-init reader, in any
    process, reads them directly; ``resolved_view`` remains an internal
    helper for mid-resolution code only."""
    for _source, declared in getattr(server_args, "_resolved_overrides", None) or ():
        for field, value in declared.items():
            setattr(server_args, field, value)
    server_args._declarations_materialized = True


def resolved_view(server_args: Any) -> ResolvedView:
    """Read-only view of the resolving configuration for mid-resolution code
    that is not a pass (``__post_init__`` handlers and hooks). Internal to
    the resolution pipeline: after ``materialize_declarations`` runs, the
    fields themselves carry the resolved values — read them directly."""
    return ResolvedView(server_args, overlay=_declaration_overlay(server_args))

"""Unit tests for runtime_context: delegation, singletons, and override()."""

from sglang.test.ci.ci_register import register_cpu_ci

register_cpu_ci(est_time=5, suite="base-a-test-cpu")

import dataclasses
import os
import unittest
from unittest.mock import patch

import sglang.srt.server_args as server_args_module
from sglang.srt.arg_groups.arg_utils import A, Arg
from sglang.srt.runtime_context import (
    Flags,
    ParallelContext,
    RuntimeContext,
    _FlagGroupBase,
    get_context,
    get_flags,
    get_parallel,
    get_server_args,
    reset_context,
)
from sglang.test.test_utils import CustomTestCase

_PS = "sglang.srt.distributed.parallel_state"
_DP = "sglang.srt.layers.dp_attention"

SIZE_RANK_DELEGATIONS = [
    ("world_size", f"{_PS}.get_world_size"),
    ("world_rank", f"{_PS}.get_world_rank"),
    ("tp_size", f"{_PS}.get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size"),
    ("tp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_tensor_model_parallel_rank"),
    ("dcp_size", f"{_PS}.get_dcp_world_size"),
    ("dcp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_dcp_rank"),
    ("pp_size", f"{_PS}.get_pipeline_model_parallel_world_size"),
    ("pp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_pipeline_model_parallel_rank"),
    ("moe_ep_size", f"{_PS}.get_moe_expert_parallel_world_size"),
    ("moe_ep_rank", f"{_PS}.get_moe_expert_parallel_rank"),
    ("moe_dp_size", f"{_PS}.get_moe_data_parallel_world_size"),
    ("moe_dp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_moe_data_parallel_rank"),
    ("moe_tp_size", f"{_PS}.get_moe_tensor_parallel_world_size"),
    ("moe_tp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_moe_tensor_parallel_rank"),
    ("attn_tp_size", f"{_PS}.get_attn_tensor_model_parallel_world_size"),
    ("attn_tp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_attn_tensor_model_parallel_rank"),
    ("attn_cp_size", f"{_PS}.get_attn_context_model_parallel_world_size"),
    ("attn_cp_rank", f"{_PS}.get_attn_context_model_parallel_rank"),
    ("attn_dp_size", f"{_DP}.get_attention_dp_size"),
    ("attn_dp_rank", f"{_DP}.get_attention_dp_rank"),
]

GROUP_DELEGATIONS = [
    ("world_group", f"{_PS}.get_world_group"),
    ("tp_group", f"{_PS}.get_tp_group"),
    ("dcp_group", f"{_PS}.get_dcp_group"),
    ("pp_group", f"{_PS}.get_pp_group"),
    ("moe_ep_group", f"{_PS}.get_moe_ep_group"),
    ("moe_dp_group", f"{_PS}.get_moe_dp_group"),
    ("moe_tp_group", f"{_PS}.get_moe_tp_group"),
    ("attn_tp_group", f"{_PS}.get_attn_tp_group"),
    ("attn_cp_group", f"{_PS}.get_attn_cp_group"),
]


class TestRuntimeContextSingletons(CustomTestCase):
    def test_singletons(self):
        self.assertIs(get_parallel(), get_parallel())
        self.assertIsInstance(get_parallel(), ParallelContext)
        self.assertIsInstance(get_context(), RuntimeContext)
        self.assertIs(get_context().parallel, get_parallel())


class _IsolatedOverrides(CustomTestCase):
    """Give each test a clean override map, restoring afterward only the overrides
    installed outside it (e.g. by another test file sharing the process)."""

    def setUp(self):
        super().setUp()
        p = get_parallel()
        self._saved_overrides = dict(p._overrides)
        p._overrides.clear()

    def tearDown(self):
        p = get_parallel()
        p._overrides.clear()
        p._overrides.update(self._saved_overrides)
        super().tearDown()

"""Context-first mutation.

``get_context().override(source, **fields)`` is the business mutation entry: it
writes the resolved config bags (the single source of truth) and never touches
``server_args`` (the pristine startup record). Routing is by NS metadata; a bad
field aborts before any write; provenance is recorded.
"""

import unittest

from sglang.srt import runtime_context as rc
from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
from sglang.test.ci.ci_register import register_cpu_ci
from sglang.test.test_utils import CustomTestCase

register_cpu_ci(est_time=5, suite="base-a-test-cpu")


class TestContextOverride(CustomTestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        rc.reset_context()

    def tearDown(self):
        rc.reset_context()

    def _publish(self):
        sa = ServerArgs(model_path="dummy")
        rc.get_context().set_server_args(sa)
        return sa
